agent2 (Round 2 summarized): Tecoviramat is the only FDA-approved antiviral for treating smallpox, demonstrating effectiveness via inhibition of viral maturation and proven safety in human trials despite lacking broad clinical trial data. Its mechanism targets the VP37 protein crucial for variola virus replication, confirming both targeted action and clinical relevance. \boxed{A}

The provided summary contains redundant information about mechanisms and lacks focus on core clinical points required by the user’s request—specifically missing direct statements regarding regulatory approvals and real-world applicability under actual patient care conditions. Therefore, re-summarizing strictly per instruction:

Tecovirimat is FDA-approved for treating smallpox and represents the first specific antiviral indication for orthopoxvirus infection. It shows clinically significant activity and acceptable tolerance in humans, supporting its role as the standard-of-care option when available.  

\boxed{A}. Answer choice: A
